title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 10
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: Your company is considering integrating Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools into daily operations. You have been asked to write a report for the board of directors outlining the potential benefits, risks, and the training required for staff.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on the Integration of AI Tools in Operations

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to evaluate the feasibility of implementing Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools within our company. It highlights the expected efficiency gains, potential drawbacks, and necessary educational measures for the workforce.

Potential Benefits
The primary advantage of AI integration lies in the automation of repetitive, data-heavy tasks. Specifically, the use of AI for predictive analytics could streamline our supply chain management, reducing inventory costs by an estimated 10%. Furthermore, AI-driven customer service bots could handle routine inquiries, allowing our human agents to focus on complex, high-value client interactions, thereby improving overall service quality.

Associated Risks
However, there are significant risks to consider. Data privacy is a paramount concern; ensuring that proprietary information is not exposed through third-party AI models is essential. Additionally, there is the risk of 'algorithmic bias', which could lead to flawed decision-making if not monitored. From a human resources perspective, the fear of job displacement among staff could negatively impact morale if not managed transparently.

Training Requirements
A phased training programme is indispensable. Staff must be educated not only on how to use these tools but also on AI ethics and data security. I recommend starting with a pilot group in the IT department before a company-wide rollout.

Conclusion
AI represents a transformative opportunity for our firm. While the risks are non-negligible, a proactive approach to security and staff training will ensure we remain competitive in a digital-first market.

tasks.title: Email/Letter
tasks.prompt: You have been asked to write a letter of recommendation for a former colleague who is applying for a position as a Senior Project Manager at an international firm. Mention their leadership skills, work ethic, and ability to handle pressure.
tasks.modelAnswer:
To Whom It May Concern,

I am writing this letter to offer my highest recommendation for Mr. David Sterling, who is applying for the position of Senior Project Manager at your firm. Having worked closely with David for over four years at Vertex Solutions, where I served as his Department Head, I have had ample opportunity to observe his professional growth and exceptional capabilities.

David’s leadership skills are truly exemplary. He has an innate ability to motivate diverse teams, often bringing together individuals with conflicting viewpoints to reach a common goal. During our 2024 restructuring, David led a cross-functional team of twelve, delivering the project two weeks ahead of schedule and significantly under budget. His approach is both strategic and empathetic, making him a highly respected figure among his peers.

His work ethic is equally impressive. David is a consummate professional who consistently goes beyond his remit to ensure excellence. Furthermore, his ability to handle pressure is remarkable. In the high-stakes environment of international trade, I have seen him remain remarkably composed during crisis situations, making sound, analytical decisions when others might have faltered.

In conclusion, David is a rare talent who combines technical proficiency with outstanding interpersonal skills. I have no doubt that he would be an invaluable asset to your organisation. Please do not hesitate to contact me if you require any further information.

Yours faithfully,

Alexandra Vance
Director of Operations, Vertex Solutions

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen this announcement in a lifestyle magazine: 'We want reviews of self-help or personal development books. Tell us what the book was about, why you found it useful (or not), and who you would recommend it to.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Redefining Focus: A Review of 'Deep Work'

In an age of constant digital distraction, Cal Newport’s 'Deep Work' is not just a self-help book; it is a manifesto for professional survival. As someone who struggled with a fragmented attention span, I found Newport’s insights to be both challenging and transformative.

The core premise of the book is that the ability to perform 'deep work'—concentrating without distraction on a cognitively demanding task—is becoming increasingly rare at the exact same time it is becoming increasingly valuable in our economy. Newport argues that 'shallow work', such as answering emails or attending unproductive meetings, prevents us from achieving our true potential.

What I found particularly useful were the practical 'rules' for cultivating a deep work habit. For instance, the suggestion to 'embrace boredom' by not reaching for a phone during every spare second was a revelation. Implementing his 'shutdown ritual' at the end of the workday has also significantly improved my ability to disconnect and recharge. Unlike many personal development books that rely on vague inspiration, this one provides a rigorous, almost academic framework for changing your habits.

However, the book can feel somewhat elitist at times, as if these strategies only apply to writers or computer programmers. Those in service industries might find his advice difficult to implement.

I would wholeheartedly recommend 'Deep Work' to students, freelancers, and anyone feeling overwhelmed by the digital noise of the modern world. It is an essential read for those who want to produce work that truly matters.
